decks.notes: This deck focuses on creating a powerful board state quickly by utilizing Amulet of Vigor to untap lands and generate extra mana. The goal is to ramp up to Primeval Titan and utilize its powerful ability to fetch lands like Gruul Turf and Simic Growth Chamber, which can then be bounced and replayed multiple times in a turn. By efficiently ramping and tutoring for key combo pieces like Scapeshift, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm opponents with powerful land-based combos and win the game in explosive fashion.

This Mardu Aggro deck focuses on aggressively deploying creatures and using cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge to control the board and deal direct damage to the opponent. Utilizing powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and efficiently. The deck also includes some removal spells like Fatal Push and Thraben Charm to deal with any threats from the opponent, while also incorporating some land destruction elements with cards like Guide of Souls and Dalkovan Encampment.

The deck focuses on aggressive play using a combination of efficient creatures like Territorial Kavu, Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, and Kroxa, Titan of Death's Hunger, as well as removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Fatal Push. It aims to control the board with Leyline Binding and Leyline of the Guildpact while maintaining pressure on the opponent. The deck also utilizes mana fixing with a mix of lands like Marsh Flats and Wooded Foothills, as well as some card draw and disruption with cards like Consign to Memory and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker.

This deck focuses on aggressively attacking opponents with creatures while also utilizing the blinking mechanic to protect and re-use key cards like Starfield Shepherd and Solitude. By constantly returning creatures to the battlefield and disrupting opponents' strategies with cards like Teferi, Time Raveler, this deck aims to overwhelm and outmaneuver opponents with a mix of aggro and control elements. The combination of efficient creatures and blink spells creates a versatile and dynamic playstyle that can adapt to various matchups in a Modern 1 vs 1 format.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, supported by efficient removal like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment. The deck also utilizes disruption with cards like Mana Tithe and Ranger-Captain of Eos to maintain board control. Additionally, powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provide additional win conditions. The mana base is streamlined with fetch lands like Arid Mesa and Flooded Strand, allowing for consistent access to the deck's powerful spells and creatures.

This deck focuses on combining aggressive creature attacks with the ability to temporarily remove threats from the battlefield through blinking effects. By utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp, the deck can repeatedly trigger enter-the-battlefield effects of creatures like Quantum Riddler and Overlord of the Balemurk. This disruption, combined with efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Solitude, allows the deck to control the board while maintaining a fast pace of damage output. Key cards like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Witch Enchanter provide additional ways to generate advantage and overwhelm the opponent.

The deck focuses on maintaining control of the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage engines. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win conditions. The inclusion of Narset, Parter of Veils and Teferi, Time Raveler helps to limit the opponent's resources and slow down their plays. By controlling the board and drawing extra cards with cards like Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive, the deck seeks to outvalue and outlast the opponent until it can secure a victory with powerful threats like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Solitude.

This UW Control deck focuses on utilizing various counterspells and removal spells to control the game, while also incorporating card advantage with Teferi and Consult the Star Charts. The deck also includes bounce effects and board wipes to maintain control of the battlefield. The addition of Isochron Scepter allows for repeating key spells, while Kaheera, the Orphanguard provides a companion with additional synergy to the deck's overall strategy. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the deck's color requirements and maximize consistency.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, efficient creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to quickly build up a board presence with aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter, while utilizing spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to control the board and finish off opponents. The deck also includes card advantage engines like Mishra's Bauble and Expressive Iteration to maintain tempo and consistency. Overall, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and end the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck aims to control the game by using powerful artifacts and Eldrazi creatures. By utilizing the Urza lands to generate a large amount of mana, the deck can quickly deploy threats like Thought-Knot Seer and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Cards like Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere help to lock down the opponent's spells, while Karn, the Great Creator provides additional utility and access to silver bullets from the sideboard. The deck's end game can be sealed with devastating Eldrazi creatures like Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the combo potential of Broodscale Bloodchief with various support cards to overwhelm the opponent. By consistently generating tokens with Blade of the Bloodchief and utilizing cards like Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble for control and removal, the deck aims to create a board state that the opponent cannot easily deal with. Additionally, the inclusion of Emrakul, the Promised End and other powerful Eldrazi creatures provides additional win conditions and late-game power. Overall, the deck is designed to create a strong and resilient combo engine that can dominate the battlefield.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive plays and utilizing efficient creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes powerful one-drop and two-drop creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, as well as impactful enchantments like Goblin Bombardment and Static Prison to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes some versatile removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Solitude to deal with threats. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to apply consistent pressure and close out games quickly with a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive elements.

The deck focuses on using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Solitude to quickly reanimate powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Ulamog, the Defiler. By utilizing Psychic Frog and Atraxa, Grand Unifier, the deck can control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategies while setting up its own combo plays. With access to counterspells like Force of Negation and disruptive cards like Thoughtseize, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and win through quick and powerful reanimation plays.

The deck focuses on utilizing mana acceleration and land tutoring in order to ramp up quickly and efficiently to play Primeval Titan and search for utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures, Hanweir Battlements, and The Mycosynth Gardens. By taking advantage of the Amulet of Vigor to untap lands, the deck can generate a large amount of mana to cast powerful spells like Scapeshift and Malevolent Rumble. The key goal is to assemble the Amulet Titan combo as quickly as possible to overwhelm the opponent with a powerful board presence and land advantage.

This deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with powerful creatures and then bringing them back to the battlefield to overwhelm the opponent. Using cards like Faithless Looting and Thought Scour to fill the graveyard, and spells like Unearth and Persist to reanimate creatures like Emperor of Bones and Archon of Cruelty, this deck aims to bring out big threats early in the game and control the board. The combination of discard spells like Thoughtseize and removal like Fatal Push also help disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up for explosive plays.

The main concept of this deck is to use a combination of cards that allow for creatures to be put into the graveyard, then reanimated onto the battlefield for a powerful advantage. Cards like Faithless Looting and Thoughtseize help with card selection and disruption, while Unearth and Persist are key pieces to bring back creatures from the graveyard. The deck includes control elements like Fatal Push and Spell Pierce to protect the combo, as well as card draw with Thought Scour. Overall, the goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful reanimated creatures.
